commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0f924baa8fb5eac1c9a6275e14c23fcbb8b34adf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Preferences tab layout coordinator, uses lightweight provider sidebar subtitles while keeping rich provider detail subtitles, adds coordinator tests, and updates CHANGELOG.md.

Reproducibility: no. source inspection shows current main resizes from selection observation after TabView selection changes, but I did not establish a live failing Settings reproduction. The PR tests model the event ordering, but they do not reproduce the visible AppKit/SwiftUI lag.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer-supplied visible Settings proof before merge; there is no safe automation lane for proving the contributor’s UI setup.

target.title: fix: smooth providers settings tab switching without waiting on resize
target.body:
## Summary

- Commit the selected settings tab before scheduling animated window resizing.
- Use a focused coordinator to suppress duplicate layout work and ignore stale rapid-click callbacks.
- Keep programmatic tab changes responsive.
- Use lightweight provider sidebar subtitles while retaining rich presentation for the selected provider detail.

## Proof

- `swift test --filter 'PreferencesTabLayoutCoordinatorTests|ProvidersPaneCoverageTests'` (32 tests passed)
- Coordinator coverage: one-layout tab clicks, same-tab no-op, programmatic selection, rapid clicks, stale observations, and interrupted deferred layout.
- `make check`
- `autoreview --mode uncommitted` (clean, confidence 0.82)
- `./Scripts/compile_and_run.sh`: exact `0f924baa` bundle built, signed, launched, and remained running.

## UI automation note

The current desktop keeps CodexBar's status item overflow-hidden. Peekaboo correctly exposed a zero-size status frame, so coordinate-click success was rejected as proof. Computer Use also timed out before obtaining app state. No Settings screenshot is claimed; deterministic coordinator tests cover the timing-sensitive behavior.
target.author: elijahfriedman

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
